issues: The draft describes only the NHL injury report, ignoring the more Sockeyes-relevant content: a Moncton Hawks vs. Saint John AHL game summary prominently featuring ex-Sockeye Dave Tomlinson (Moncton), who scored a hat trick (goals 16, 17, 18) and was named first star.

review.correctedDescriptionShort: Ex-Sockeye Dave Tomlinson scored a hat trick and was named first star as the Moncton Hawks defeated the Saint John Flames 8-4 in AHL action on December 8, 1993.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The page contains an AHL game summary from December 8, 1993 in which former Richmond Sockeyes player Dave Tomlinson, playing for the Moncton Hawks, scored three goals (his 16th, 17th, and 18th of the season) and was named first star of the game in an 8-4 win over the Saint John Flames. Also present on the page is an NHL injury and roster update report that notes Tomlinson had been reassigned to Moncton (AHL) by the Winnipeg Jets on December 8, 1993. Together the two items document Tomlinson's AHL activity immediately following his NHL roster move.
review.notes: The AI draft focused entirely on the NHL injury report, missing the more archivally significant AHL game summary in which ex-Sockeye Dave Tomlinson scored a hat trick for Moncton and earned first-star honours. Both items on the page are relevant, but the game summary is the primary Sockeyes-relevant content and should lead the description.
